description: Nitrate sensing and signaling pathway via the NRT1.1/NLP7 module
transduction_chain: Nitrate binds NRT1.1 in root epidermis
CIPK8/CIPK23 phosphorylates NRT1.1, switching affinity mode
Calcium signaling via CPK10/CPK30/CPK32
NLP7 transcription factor activated (nuclear retention under nitrate)
NLP7 binds nitrate-responsive cis-elements (NRE) in target promoters
Activation of NRT2.1, NIA1, NIA2, and other nitrate-responsive genes
notes: Well-characterized in Arabidopsis. NRT2.1 promoter shows strong induction under low nitrate (0.2 mM) and repression under high N. Response time: 2-6 hours.

description: ABA-dependent and ABA-independent drought stress signaling
receptor: PYR/PYL/RCAR ABA receptors (ABA-dependent) and membrane osmosensors (ABA-independent)
transduction_chain: Water deficit triggers ABA biosynthesis in roots and leaves
ABA binds PYR/PYL/RCAR receptors
PP2C phosphatases (ABI1, ABI2) inhibited
SnRK2 kinases activated (OST1/SnRK2.6)
SnRK2 phosphorylates AREB/ABF transcription factors
AREB/ABF bind ABRE cis-elements in stress-responsive promoters
ABA-independent: DREB2A binds DRE/CRT elements via DREB2A pathway
notes: RD29A promoter contains both DRE and ABRE elements, making it responsive to drought, cold, and ABA. Strong induction within 1-3 hours of dehydration.

description: Metal-responsive signaling via metallothioneins and metal-responsive elements
receptor: Metal ion importers (IRT1 for Fe/Zn/Cd, COPT1 for Cu) and intracellular metal sensors
transduction_chain: Heavy metal ions (Cu²⁺, Zn²⁺, Cd²⁺) enter cells via metal transporters
Free metal ions detected by metal-sensing transcription factors
Metal-responsive transcription factors bind MRE (Metal Response Elements)
Activation of metallothionein genes (MT1A, MT2A, MT2B, MT3)
Phytochelatin synthase (PCS1) activated for chelation
Detoxification via vacuolar sequestration (HMA3, MTP1)
notes: MT2A responds to Cu, Zn, and Cd. Specificity depends on metal concentration range. For field applications, consider threshold sensitivity — low specificity between different metals.

description: Phosphate starvation response via PHR1/SPX signaling module
receptor: Local sensing at root tip (uncertain) and systemic signaling via SPX domain proteins
transduction_chain: Low Pi detected — SPX proteins release PHR1 (PHOSPHATE STARVATION RESPONSE 1)
PHR1 (MYB-CC transcription factor) accumulates in nucleus
PHR1 binds P1BS (PHR1 Binding Sequence: GNATATNC) in target promoters
Upregulation of phosphate transporters (PHT1;1-PHT1;4)
miR399 induced, targets PHO2 for degradation
Systemic signaling: shoot-to-root mobile miR399 amplifies Pi starvation response
notes: PHT1;1 promoter is strongly root-preferential. High induction under Pi starvation (<10 µM Pi) with 6-24 hour response time. Excellent dynamic range.
